subject: [PATCH v6 6/6] docs: fuse: document io-uring buffer pool and zero-copy uapi

2026-07-16.patch_summary: This patch adds documentation to the Linux kernel's FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) subsystem, specifically covering its usage of io-uring buffer pools and zero-copy functionality. The new documentation explains how buffer pools decouple entries from payload buffers, allowing for more efficient memory management and reducing overhead. Additionally, it describes zero-copy, a feature that enables direct I/O operations between the server and client's pages without an intermediary copy, requiring CAP_SYS_ADMIN privileges. This patch aims to improve understanding of FUSE over io-uring usage and its capabilities.
